title: Stop Counting Tokens: OpenAI Releases the Scorecard for the AI Era, Calculating ROI per Dollar of Useful Intelligence for CFOs
excerpt: When chief financial officers around the world are asking the same question, OpenAI decided to take matters into its own hands and figure out the answer: exactl
content_html:
When chief financial officers around the world are asking the same question, OpenAI decided to take matters into its own hands and figure out the answer: exactly how much value have we gained from our investment in artificial intelligence? Over the past decade, the software industry has measured success by adoption—how many licenses were sold, how many active users there are, and how many licenses were renewed. But with AI, that yardstick no longer works. The real measure should be how much work the model actually gets done.

In a long article released on July 17, OpenAI laid out the core economic challenge facing business leaders: Is the value generated by AI tasks growing faster than the cost of producing them? To answer this, just looking at the cost per token is not enough. A cheaper model may have a lower price per token, but it might require more trial and error, take more time, and involve more manual review to get good results; a more expensive model may have a higher price per token, but it could complete the task correctly on the first try. The real cost is the total expense of achieving a successful outcome, compared to the value that outcome generates.

Thus, OpenAI introduced the ultimate scorecard for the AI era: Useful Intelligence per Dollar. This metric answers four questions: Is AI completing meaningful work? What is the cost of each successful task? Can people trust the results? As usage grows, does each dollar spent on AI create more value?

First, how much useful work has been completed. Value is only created when tokens are transformed into actual, directly usable outputs. The stronger the model, the longer and more complex the tasks it can handle: it starts maintaining context, performing multi-step reasoning, collaborating across tools, and continuously adapting along the way. The most practical approach is to lock onto a specific workflow, clearly define what "completion" means, and then measure the result within the system where the work actually happens. For support teams, completion means a customer issue is resolved; for engineering teams, it's a code change that passes tests; for legal teams, it's an accurately and timely reviewed contract. OpenAI gave an example of a finance team preparing for a forecast review: before the final decision, they need to find the latest forecast, import data into Excel or Sheets, identify changes, check tabs, rebuild slides, and verify all numbers align—a series of tedious tasks that can now be handled by ChatGPT Work, allowing the team to focus on what truly matters—what changed, why, and what comes next. This is the useful intelligence you get for every dollar spent in practice.

Second, how much did a successful task actually cost. The cost of AI tasks varies widely: a quick answer consumes little computation, while coding, research, and financial workflows often involve deep reasoning, tool calls, and extensive operations, consuming more compute and creating greater value. At the model level, the cost of a single successful task depends on price, actual compute used, and the probability of getting the correct result; for companies, total cost also includes employee time, manual review, retries, and rework. The formula is simple: add up the total cost of completing the work and divide by the number of tasks meeting quality standards. This explains why the lowest cost per token doesn’t always lead to the lowest cost per result— even for routine requests, if a cutting-edge model gives the right answer on the first try, the savings on retries, delays, reviews, and total compute may make it the most cost-effective choice.

OpenAI’s recently released GPT-5.6 was designed according to this logic, with three tiers: Sol is the flagship, Terra balances performance and cost, and Luna is the fastest and most efficient. This tiered approach provides customers with a starting point for optimization, but OpenAI emphasizes that the final choice of model should depend on the overall economics of the task—use Luna for high-throughput processes, Terra for deeper tasks, and Sol when stronger reasoning can achieve better results with fewer attempts. During the training of GPT-5.6, OpenAI aimed to increase the amount of useful output per token: in the Artificial Analysis programming intelligence index, the GPT-5.6 Sol achieved new best levels with maximum reasoning, while using 54% fewer output tokens than another leading model; in the DeepSWE v1.1 long-cycle engineering tasks, GPT-5.6 Sol reached a new high of 72.7%, surpassing Claude Fable5's 69.9%, with an estimated API cost reduction of 36.2%. Each generation of models should improve in two areas—higher efficiency makes old tasks cheaper, and stronger capabilities make entirely new types of work possible.

Third, how frequently AI completes tasks correctly, which is reliability. The adoption of AI typically follows stages of deepening: first, it assists in drafting, then finds context between tools and data, performs reasoning, and later takes proactive actions, handles exceptions, and runs full workflows, with humans only stepping in when necessary for judgment and control. Each step creates more value but also raises system requirements. Reliability itself is money—when results are accurate, reliable, consistent, and appropriately escalated, the time spent on review, correction, and rework decreases, reducing the cost of successful tasks and giving organizations more confidence in integrating AI into more critical processes.

OpenAI suggests teams track three types of results to measure this: directly usable, requiring corrections, or requiring escalation. This approach better reflects whether AI is truly reducing the workload required to complete projects than just measuring model accuracy. Reliability also requires clear boundaries: before AI moves from drafting to action, organizations must define what data the system can access, what systems it can use or modify, and when human review or approval is needed for an operation. Security, safeguards, privacy, and control form the foundation for deep usage, and ChatGPT Work is built on the security, compliance, and workspace management of ChatGPT Enterprise, allowing organizations to maintain oversight while enabling AI to access more valuable processes. Ability brings the first use, but reliability makes AI a component of the work itself.

Fourth, as usage grows, can each dollar invested in AI complete more work? Companies can track the same workflow over time to measure: count the number of tasks meeting quality standards, the total cost to complete them, and the cost per successful task. If the amount of work completed grows faster than the total cost, and quality remains the same or improves, then each dollar produces more value. Compute lies at the heart of this equation—it drives research and powers every AI task, determining product quality, speed, reliability, availability, and cost. Training compute builds future capabilities, and inference compute delivers current value, both ultimately translating into better customer outcomes.

More advanced models, more efficient inference, specialized hardware, higher utilization, smarter routing, and stronger product design can all improve compute returns. Customers feel these improvements: answers are more accurate, responses are faster, corrections are fewer, products are more reliable, and the cost of completing tasks is lower. These benefits compound—better infrastructure accelerates research, research leads to stronger and more efficient models, model improvements enhance products, and product growth drives adoption, learning, and revenue growth, which in turn supports continued investment in the next generation of research, compute, deployment, and security. OpenAI unifies all these elements under a single intelligent platform: users access it through ChatGPT and ChatGPT Work, developers build via Codex and APIs, and enterprises deploy it into real-world systems where work happens. Any improvement at any layer benefits all products and customers.

Combining these four metrics, this scorecard actually answers one thing: is the useful intelligence gained per unit of cost continuing to rise? Useful output tells us what AI can produce, the cost of each successful task tells us what it costs to achieve results, reliability tells us how much of the output people can confidently use, and scalability value shows whether each dollar and unit of compute achieves more over time. OpenAI sees its responsibility as making this equation better with each generation of models—stronger models, faster and more reliable results, and lower costs for the work customers truly need. When AI starts speaking in terms of useful intelligence per dollar rather than token counts, only then is value truly calculated.

blocks.html_en: When chief financial officers around the world are asking the same question, OpenAI decided to take matters into its own hands and figure out the answer: exactly how much value have we gained from our investment in artificial intelligence? Over the past decade, the software industry has measured success by adoption—how many licenses were sold, how many active users there are, and how many licenses were renewed. sentences.en: When chief financial officers around the world are asking the same question, OpenAI decided to take matters into its own hands and figure out the answer: exactly how much value have we gained from our investment in artificial intelligence?
sentences.zh: 当世界各地的首席财务官都在问同样的问题时，OpenAI决定亲自解决问题并找出答案：我们从人工智能的投资中到底获得了多少价值？

sentences.en: Over the past decade, the software industry has measured success by adoption—how many licenses were sold, how many active users there are, and how many licenses were renewed.
sentences.zh: 在过去的十年中，软件行业通过采用来衡量成功——销售了多少许可证、有多少活跃用户以及续订了多少许可证。

sentences.en: But with AI, that yardstick no longer works.
sentences.zh: 但对于人工智能来说，这个标准不再适用。

sentences.en: The real measure should be how much work the model actually gets done.
sentences.zh: 真正的衡量标准应该是模型实际完成了多少工作。

blocks.html_en: In a long article released on July 17, OpenAI laid out the core economic challenge facing business leaders: Is the value generated by AI tasks growing faster than the cost of producing them? To answer this, just looking at the cost per token is not enough. A cheaper model may have a lower price per token, but it might require more trial and error, take more time, and involve more manual review to get good results; a more expensive model may have a higher price per token, but it could complete the task correctly on the first try. The real cost is the total expense of achieving a successful outcome, compared to the value that outcome generates.

sentences.en: In a long article released on July 17, OpenAI laid out the core economic challenge facing business leaders: Is the value generated by AI tasks growing faster than the cost of producing them?
sentences.zh: 在 7 月 17 日发布的长文中，OpenAI 阐述了企业领导者面临的核心经济挑战：AI 任务产生的价值增长速度是否快于生产这些任务的成本增长速度？

sentences.en: To answer this, just looking at the cost per token is not enough.
sentences.zh: 要回答这个问题，仅仅查看每个代币的成本是不够的。

sentences.en: A cheaper model may have a lower price per token, but it might require more trial and error, take more time, and involve more manual review to get good results; a more expensive model may have a higher price per token, but it could complete the task correctly on the first try.
sentences.zh: 更便宜的模型可能每个代币的价格较低，但可能需要更多的试验和错误，需要更多的时间，并涉及更多的人工审核才能获得良好的结果；更昂贵的模型可能每个代币的价格更高，但它可以在第一次尝试时正确完成任务。

sentences.en: The real cost is the total expense of achieving a successful outcome, compared to the value that outcome generates.
sentences.zh: 实际成本是与结果产生的价值相比，实现成功结果的总费用。

blocks.html_en: Thus, OpenAI introduced the ultimate scorecard for the AI era: Useful Intelligence per Dollar. This metric answers four questions: Is AI completing meaningful work? What is the cost of each successful task? Can people trust the results? As usage grows, does each dollar spent on AI create more value?

sentences.en: Thus, OpenAI introduced the ultimate scorecard for the AI era: Useful Intelligence per Dollar.
sentences.zh: 因此，OpenAI 推出了人工智能时代的终极记分卡：每美元有用的智能。

sentences.en: This metric answers four questions: Is AI completing meaningful work?
sentences.zh: 该指标回答了四个问题：人工智能是否完成了有意义的工作？

sentences.en: What is the cost of each successful task?
sentences.zh: 每项成功任务的成本是多少？

sentences.en: Can people trust the results?
sentences.zh: 人们可以相信结果吗？

sentences.en: As usage grows, does each dollar spent on AI create more value?
sentences.zh: 随着使用量的增长，花在人工智能上的每一美元都会创造更多价值吗？

sentences.en: First, how much useful work has been completed.
sentences.zh: 首先，完成了多少有用的工作。

sentences.en: Value is only created when tokens are transformed into actual, directly usable outputs.
sentences.zh: 只有当代币转化为实际的、可直接使用的输出时，价值才会被创造。

sentences.en: The stronger the model, the longer and more complex the tasks it can handle: it starts maintaining context, performing multi-step reasoning, collaborating across tools, and continuously adapting along the way.
sentences.zh: 模型越强大，它可以处理的任务越长、越复杂：它开始维护上下文、执行多步骤推理、跨工具协作，并在此过程中不断适应。

sentences.en: The most practical approach is to lock onto a specific workflow, clearly define what "completion" means, and then measure the result within the system where the work actually happens.
sentences.zh: 最实用的方法是锁定特定的工作流程，明确定义“完成”的含义，然后在实际发生工作的系统内衡量结果。

sentences.en: For support teams, completion means a customer issue is resolved; for engineering teams, it's a code change that passes tests; for legal teams, it's an accurately and timely reviewed contract.
sentences.zh: 对于支持团队来说，完成意味着客户问题得到解决；对于工程团队来说，这是通过测试的代码更改；对于法律团队来说，这是一份经过准确、及时审查的合同。

sentences.en: OpenAI gave an example of a finance team preparing for a forecast review: before the final decision, they need to find the latest forecast, import data into Excel or Sheets, identify changes, check tabs, rebuild slides, and verify all numbers align—a series of tedious tasks that can now be handled by ChatGPT Work, allowing the team to focus on what truly matters—what changed, why, and what comes next.
sentences.zh: OpenAI 举了一个财务团队准备预测审查的例子：在做出最终决定之前，他们需要找到最新的预测，将数据导入 Excel 或表格，识别更改，检查选项卡，重建幻灯片，并验证所有数字是否对齐——一系列繁琐的任务现在可以由 ChatGPT Work 来处理，从而使团队能够专注于真正重要的事情——发生了什么变化、为什么发生变化以及接下来会发生什么。

sentences.en: This is the useful intelligence you get for every dollar spent in practice.
sentences.zh: 这是您在实践中花费每一美元所获得的有用情报。

sentences.en: Second, how much did a successful task actually cost.
sentences.zh: 其次，一项成功的任务实际花费了多少钱。

sentences.en: The cost of AI tasks varies widely: a quick answer consumes little computation, while coding, research, and financial workflows often involve deep reasoning, tool calls, and extensive operations, consuming more compute and creating greater value.
sentences.zh: 人工智能任务的成本差异很大：快速回答只消耗很少的计算量，而编码、研究和财务工作流程通常涉及深度推理、工具调用和大量操作，消耗更多的计算量并创造更大的价值。

sentences.en: At the model level, the cost of a single successful task depends on price, actual compute used, and the probability of getting the correct result; for companies, total cost also includes employee time, manual review, retries, and rework.
sentences.zh: 在模型层面，单个成功任务的成本取决于价格、实际使用的计算以及获得正确结果的概率；对于公司来说，总成本还包括员工时间、人工审核、重试和返工。

sentences.en: The formula is simple: add up the total cost of completing the work and divide by the number of tasks meeting quality standards.
sentences.zh: 公式很简单：将完成工作的总成本加起来，除以符合质量标准的任务数量。

sentences.en: This explains why the lowest cost per token doesn’t always lead to the lowest cost per result— even for routine requests, if a cutting-edge model gives the right answer on the first try, the savings on retries, delays, reviews, and total compute may make it the most cost-effective choice.
sentences.zh: 这解释了为什么每个令牌的最低成本并不总是导致每个结果的最低成本——即使对于常规请求，如果尖端模型在第一次尝试时给出了正确的答案，那么重试、延迟、审查和总计算方面的节省可能会使其成为最具成本效益的选择。

sentences.zh: OpenAI最近发布的GPT-5.6就是按照这个逻辑设计的，分为三层：Sol是旗舰，Terra平衡性能和成本，Luna是最快和最高效的。

sentences.zh: 这种分层方法为客户提供了优化的起点，但 OpenAI 强调模型的最终选择应取决于任务的整体经济性——对于高吞吐量流程使用 Luna，对于更深层次的任务使用 Terra，当更强的推理可以通过更少的尝试获得更好的结果时使用 Sol。

sentences.en: During the training of GPT-5.6, OpenAI aimed to increase the amount of useful output per token: in the Artificial Analysis programming intelligence index, the GPT-5.6 Sol achieved new best levels with maximum reasoning, while using 54% fewer output tokens than another leading model; in the DeepSWE v1.1 long-cycle engineering tasks, GPT-5.6 Sol reached a new high of 72.7%, surpassing Claude Fable5's 69.9%, with an estimated API cost reduction of 36.2%.
sentences.zh: 在 GPT-5.6 的训练过程中，OpenAI 的目标是增加每个 token 的有用输出量：在人工分析编程智能指数中，GPT-5.6 Sol 以最大推理实现了新的最佳水平，同时使用的输出 token 比另一个领先模型少了 54%；在DeepSWE v1.1长周期工程任务中，GPT-5.6 Sol达到了72.7%的新高，超越了Claude Fable5的69.9%，预计API成本降低了36.2%。

sentences.en: Each generation of models should improve in two areas—higher efficiency makes old tasks cheaper, and stronger capabilities make entirely new types of work possible.
sentences.zh: 每一代模型都应该在两个方面进行改进——更高的效率使旧任务更便宜，而更强的功能使全新的工作类型成为可能。

Each step creates more value but also raises system requirements. Reliability itself is money—when results are accurate, reliable, consistent, and appropriately escalated, the time spent on review, correction, and rework decreases, reducing the cost of successful tasks and giving organizations more confidence in integrating AI into more critical processes.

sentences.en: Third, how frequently AI completes tasks correctly, which is reliability.
sentences.zh: 第三，人工智能正确完成任务的频率，这就是可靠性。

sentences.en: The adoption of AI typically follows stages of deepening: first, it assists in drafting, then finds context between tools and data, performs reasoning, and later takes proactive actions, handles exceptions, and runs full workflows, with humans only stepping in when necessary for judgment and control.
sentences.zh: 人工智能的采用通常遵循深化阶段：首先协助起草，然后找到工具和数据之间的上下文、进行推理，然后采取主动行动、处理异常并运行完整的工作流程，只有在必要时才由人类介入进行判断和控制。

sentences.en: Each step creates more value but also raises system requirements.
sentences.zh: 每一步都会创造更多价值，但也会提高系统要求。

sentences.en: Reliability itself is money—when results are accurate, reliable, consistent, and appropriately escalated, the time spent on review, correction, and rework decreases, reducing the cost of successful tasks and giving organizations more confidence in integrating AI into more critical processes.
sentences.zh: 可靠性本身就是金钱——当结果准确、可靠、一致且适当升级时，用于审查、纠正和返工的时间就会减少，从而降低成功任务的成本，并使组织更有信心将人工智能集成到更关键的流程中。

blocks.html_en: OpenAI suggests teams track three types of results to measure this: directly usable, requiring corrections, or requiring escalation. This approach better reflects whether AI is truly reducing the workload required to complete projects than just measuring model accuracy. Reliability also requires clear boundaries: before AI moves from drafting to action, organizations must define what data the system can access, what systems it can use or modify, and when human review or approval is needed for an operation. Security, safeguards, privacy, and control form the foundation for deep usage, and ChatGPT Work is built on the security, compliance, and workspace management of ChatGPT Enterprise, allowing organizations to maintain oversight while enabling AI to access more valuable processes. Ability brings the first use, but reliability makes AI a component of the work itself.

sentences.en: OpenAI suggests teams track three types of results to measure this: directly usable, requiring corrections, or requiring escalation.
sentences.zh: OpenAI 建议团队跟踪三种类型的结果来衡量这一点：直接可用、需要更正或需要升级。

sentences.en: This approach better reflects whether AI is truly reducing the workload required to complete projects than just measuring model accuracy.
sentences.zh: 这种方法比仅仅测量模型准确性更好地反映了人工智能是否真正减少了完成项目所需的工作量。

sentences.en: Reliability also requires clear boundaries: before AI moves from drafting to action, organizations must define what data the system can access, what systems it can use or modify, and when human review or approval is needed for an operation.
sentences.zh: 可靠性还需要明确的界限：在人工智能从起草到行动之前，组织必须定义系统可以访问哪些数据、可以使用或修改哪些系统，以及操作何时需要人工审查或批准。

sentences.en: Security, safeguards, privacy, and control form the foundation for deep usage, and ChatGPT Work is built on the security, compliance, and workspace management of ChatGPT Enterprise, allowing organizations to maintain oversight while enabling AI to access more valuable processes.
sentences.zh: 安全性、保障、隐私和控制构成了深度使用的基础，ChatGPT Work 建立在 ChatGPT Enterprise 的安全性、合规性和工作区管理之上，使组织能够保持监督，同时使 AI 能够访问更有价值的流程。

sentences.en: Ability brings the first use, but reliability makes AI a component of the work itself.
sentences.zh: 能力带来先用，但可靠性让人工智能成为工作本身的组成部分。

blocks.html_en: Fourth, as usage grows, can each dollar invested in AI complete more work? Companies can track the same workflow over time to measure: count the number of tasks meeting quality standards, the total cost to complete them, and the cost per successful task. If the amount of work completed grows faster than the total cost, and quality remains the same or improves, then each dollar produces more value. Compute lies at the heart of this equation—it drives research and powers every AI task, determining product quality, speed, reliability, availability, and cost. Training compute builds future capabilities, and inference compute delivers current value, both ultimately translating into better customer outcomes.

sentences.en: Fourth, as usage grows, can each dollar invested in AI complete more work?
sentences.zh: 第四，随着使用量的增长，投入人工智能的每一美元能否完成更多的工作？

sentences.en: Companies can track the same workflow over time to measure: count the number of tasks meeting quality standards, the total cost to complete them, and the cost per successful task.
sentences.zh: 公司可以随着时间的推移跟踪相同的工作流程来进行衡量：计算满足质量标准的任务数量、完成这些任务的总成本以及每个成功任务的成本。

sentences.en: If the amount of work completed grows faster than the total cost, and quality remains the same or improves, then each dollar produces more value.
sentences.zh: 如果完成的工作量的增长速度快于总成本的增长速度，并且质量保持不变或有所提高，那么每一美元都会产生更多的价值。

sentences.en: Compute lies at the heart of this equation—it drives research and powers every AI task, determining product quality, speed, reliability, availability, and cost.
sentences.zh: 计算是这个方程式的核心——它推动研究并为每项人工智能任务提供动力，决定产品质量、速度、可靠性、可用性和成本。

sentences.en: Training compute builds future capabilities, and inference compute delivers current value, both ultimately translating into better customer outcomes.
sentences.zh: 训练计算构建未来能力，推理计算提供当前价值，两者最终都会转化为更好的客户成果。

blocks.html_en: More advanced models, more efficient inference, specialized hardware, higher utilization, smarter routing, and stronger product design can all improve compute returns. Customers feel these improvements: answers are more accurate, responses are faster, corrections are fewer, products are more reliable, and the cost of completing tasks is lower. These benefits compound—better infrastructure accelerates research, research leads to stronger and more efficient models, model improvements enhance products, and product growth drives adoption, learning, and revenue growth, which in turn supports continued investment in the next generation of research, compute, deployment, and security. OpenAI unifies all these elements under a single intelligent platform: users access it through ChatGPT and ChatGPT Work, developers build via Codex and APIs, and enterprises deploy it into real-world systems where work happens. Any improvement at any layer benefits all products and customers.

sentences.en: More advanced models, more efficient inference, specialized hardware, higher utilization, smarter routing, and stronger product design can all improve compute returns.
sentences.zh: 更先进的模型、更高效的推理、专用硬件、更高的利用率、更智能的路由和更强大的产品设计都可以提高计算回报。

sentences.en: Customers feel these improvements: answers are more accurate, responses are faster, corrections are fewer, products are more reliable, and the cost of completing tasks is lower.
sentences.zh: 客户感受到这些改进：答案更准确、响应更快、修正更少、产品更可靠、完成任务的成本更低。

sentences.en: These benefits compound—better infrastructure accelerates research, research leads to stronger and more efficient models, model improvements enhance products, and product growth drives adoption, learning, and revenue growth, which in turn supports continued investment in the next generation of research, compute, deployment, and security.
sentences.zh: 这些好处是复合的——更好的基础设施加速研究，研究带来更强大、更高效的模型，模型改进增强产品，产品增长推动采用、学习和收入增长，这反过来又支持对下一代研究、计算、部署和安全的持续投资。

sentences.en: OpenAI unifies all these elements under a single intelligent platform: users access it through ChatGPT and ChatGPT Work, developers build via Codex and APIs, and enterprises deploy it into real-world systems where work happens.
sentences.zh: OpenAI 将所有这些元素统一在一个智能平台下：用户通过 ChatGPT 和 ChatGPT Work 访问它，开发人员通过 Codex 和 API 进行构建，企业将其部署到发生工作的现实系统中。

sentences.en: Any improvement at any layer benefits all products and customers.
sentences.zh: 任何层面的任何改进都会使所有产品和客户受益。

blocks.html_en: Combining these four metrics, this scorecard actually answers one thing: is the useful intelligence gained per unit of cost continuing to rise? Useful output tells us what AI can produce, the cost of each successful task tells us what it costs to achieve results, reliability tells us how much of the output people can confidently use, and scalability value shows whether each dollar and unit of compute achieves more over time. OpenAI sees its responsibility as making this equation better with each generation of models—stronger models, faster and more reliable results, and lower costs for the work customers truly need. When AI starts speaking in terms of useful intelligence per dollar rather than token counts, only then is value truly calculated.

sentences.en: Combining these four metrics, this scorecard actually answers one thing: is the useful intelligence gained per unit of cost continuing to rise?
sentences.zh: 结合这四个指标，这个记分卡实际上回答了一件事：每单位成本获得的有用情报是否持续上升？

sentences.en: Useful output tells us what AI can produce, the cost of each successful task tells us what it costs to achieve results, reliability tells us how much of the output people can confidently use, and scalability value shows whether each dollar and unit of compute achieves more over time.
sentences.zh: 有用的输出告诉我们人工智能可以产生什么，每项成功任务的成本告诉我们实现结果的成本，可靠性告诉我们人们可以自信地使用多少输出，可扩展性价值显示每一美元和计算单位是否随着时间的推移取得更多成果。

sentences.en: OpenAI sees its responsibility as making this equation better with each generation of models—stronger models, faster and more reliable results, and lower costs for the work customers truly need.
sentences.zh: OpenAI 认为自己的责任是让每一代模型都变得更好——更强大的模型、更快、更可靠的结果，以及更低的成本来满足客户真正需要的工作。

sentences.en: When AI starts speaking in terms of useful intelligence per dollar rather than token counts, only then is value truly calculated.
sentences.zh: 当人工智能开始用每美元的有用智能而不是代币数量来说话时，只有那时价值才真正被计算出来。

content_html_zh:
当世界各地的首席财务官都在问同样的问题时，OpenAI决定亲自解决问题并找出答案：我们从人工智能的投资中到底获得了多少价值？ 在过去的十年中，软件行业通过采用来衡量成功——销售了多少许可证、有多少活跃用户以及续订了多少许可证。 但对于人工智能来说，这个标准不再适用。 真正的衡量标准应该是模型实际完成了多少工作。

在 7 月 17 日发布的长文中，OpenAI 阐述了企业领导者面临的核心经济挑战：AI 任务产生的价值增长速度是否快于生产这些任务的成本增长速度？ 要回答这个问题，仅仅查看每个代币的成本是不够的。 更便宜的模型可能每个代币的价格较低，但可能需要更多的试验和错误，需要更多的时间，并涉及更多的人工审核才能获得良好的结果；更昂贵的模型可能每个代币的价格更高，但它可以在第一次尝试时正确完成任务。 实际成本是与结果产生的价值相比，实现成功结果的总费用。

因此，OpenAI 推出了人工智能时代的终极记分卡：每美元有用的智能。 该指标回答了四个问题：人工智能是否完成了有意义的工作？ 每项成功任务的成本是多少？ 人们可以相信结果吗？ 随着使用量的增长，花在人工智能上的每一美元都会创造更多价值吗？

首先，完成了多少有用的工作。 只有当代币转化为实际的、可直接使用的输出时，价值才会被创造。 模型越强大，它可以处理的任务越长、越复杂：它开始维护上下文、执行多步骤推理、跨工具协作，并在此过程中不断适应。 最实用的方法是锁定特定的工作流程，明确定义“完成”的含义，然后在实际发生工作的系统内衡量结果。 对于支持团队来说，完成意味着客户问题得到解决；对于工程团队来说，这是通过测试的代码更改；对于法律团队来说，这是一份经过准确、及时审查的合同。 OpenAI 举了一个财务团队准备预测审查的例子：在做出最终决定之前，他们需要找到最新的预测，将数据导入 Excel 或表格，识别更改，检查选项卡，重建幻灯片，并验证所有数字是否对齐——一系列繁琐的任务现在可以由 ChatGPT Work 来处理，从而使团队能够专注于真正重要的事情——发生了什么变化、为什么发生变化以及接下来会发生什么。 这是您在实践中花费每一美元所获得的有用情报。

其次，一项成功的任务实际花费了多少钱。 人工智能任务的成本差异很大：快速回答只消耗很少的计算量，而编码、研究和财务工作流程通常涉及深度推理、工具调用和大量操作，消耗更多的计算量并创造更大的价值。 在模型层面，单个成功任务的成本取决于价格、实际使用的计算以及获得正确结果的概率；对于公司来说，总成本还包括员工时间、人工审核、重试和返工。 公式很简单：将完成工作的总成本加起来，除以符合质量标准的任务数量。 这解释了为什么每个令牌的最低成本并不总是导致每个结果的最低成本——即使对于常规请求，如果尖端模型在第一次尝试时给出了正确的答案，那么重试、延迟、审查和总计算方面的节省可能会使其成为最具成本效益的选择。

OpenAI最近发布的GPT-5.6就是按照这个逻辑设计的，分为三层：Sol是旗舰，Terra平衡性能和成本，Luna是最快和最高效的。 这种分层方法为客户提供了优化的起点，但 OpenAI 强调模型的最终选择应取决于任务的整体经济性——对于高吞吐量流程使用 Luna，对于更深层次的任务使用 Terra，当更强的推理可以通过更少的尝试获得更好的结果时使用 Sol。 在 GPT-5.6 的训练过程中，OpenAI 的目标是增加每个 token 的有用输出量：在人工分析编程智能指数中，GPT-5.6 Sol 以最大推理实现了新的最佳水平，同时使用的输出 token 比另一个领先模型少了 54%；在DeepSWE v1.1长周期工程任务中，GPT-5.6 Sol达到了72.7%的新高，超越了Claude Fable5的69.9%，预计API成本降低了36.2%。 每一代模型都应该在两个方面进行改进——更高的效率使旧任务更便宜，而更强的功能使全新的工作类型成为可能。

第三，人工智能正确完成任务的频率，这就是可靠性。 人工智能的采用通常遵循深化阶段：首先协助起草，然后找到工具和数据之间的上下文、进行推理，然后采取主动行动、处理异常并运行完整的工作流程，只有在必要时才由人类介入进行判断和控制。 每一步都会创造更多价值，但也会提高系统要求。 可靠性本身就是金钱——当结果准确、可靠、一致且适当升级时，用于审查、纠正和返工的时间就会减少，从而降低成功任务的成本，并使组织更有信心将人工智能集成到更关键的流程中。

OpenAI 建议团队跟踪三种类型的结果来衡量这一点：直接可用、需要更正或需要升级。 这种方法比仅仅测量模型准确性更好地反映了人工智能是否真正减少了完成项目所需的工作量。 可靠性还需要明确的界限：在人工智能从起草到行动之前，组织必须定义系统可以访问哪些数据、可以使用或修改哪些系统，以及操作何时需要人工审查或批准。 安全性、保障、隐私和控制构成了深度使用的基础，ChatGPT Work 建立在 ChatGPT Enterprise 的安全性、合规性和工作区管理之上，使组织能够保持监督，同时使 AI 能够访问更有价值的流程。 能力带来先用，但可靠性让人工智能成为工作本身的组成部分。

第四，随着使用量的增长，投入人工智能的每一美元能否完成更多的工作？ 公司可以随着时间的推移跟踪相同的工作流程来进行衡量：计算满足质量标准的任务数量、完成这些任务的总成本以及每个成功任务的成本。 如果完成的工作量的增长速度快于总成本的增长速度，并且质量保持不变或有所提高，那么每一美元都会产生更多的价值。 计算是这个方程式的核心——它推动研究并为每项人工智能任务提供动力，决定产品质量、速度、可靠性、可用性和成本。 训练计算构建未来能力，推理计算提供当前价值，两者最终都会转化为更好的客户成果。

更先进的模型、更高效的推理、专用硬件、更高的利用率、更智能的路由和更强大的产品设计都可以提高计算回报。 客户感受到这些改进：答案更准确、响应更快、修正更少、产品更可靠、完成任务的成本更低。 这些好处是复合的——更好的基础设施加速研究，研究带来更强大、更高效的模型，模型改进增强产品，产品增长推动采用、学习和收入增长，这反过来又支持对下一代研究、计算、部署和安全的持续投资。 OpenAI 将所有这些元素统一在一个智能平台下：用户通过 ChatGPT 和 ChatGPT Work 访问它，开发人员通过 Codex 和 API 进行构建，企业将其部署到发生工作的现实系统中。 任何层面的任何改进都会使所有产品和客户受益。

结合这四个指标，这个记分卡实际上回答了一件事：每单位成本获得的有用情报是否持续上升？ 有用的输出告诉我们人工智能可以产生什么，每项成功任务的成本告诉我们实现结果的成本，可靠性告诉我们人们可以自信地使用多少输出，可扩展性价值显示每一美元和计算单位是否随着时间的推移取得更多成果。 OpenAI 认为自己的责任是让每一代模型都变得更好——更强大的模型、更快、更可靠的结果，以及更低的成本来满足客户真正需要的工作。 当人工智能开始用每美元的有用智能而不是代币数量来说话时，只有那时价值才真正被计算出来。
